ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for beef siew mai with caviar; 'dumpling food product' (FOODON_03315595) is the closest parent category covering steamed filled dumplings.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dim sum — steamed dumplings (siu mai type)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers steamed dim sum dumplings as the closest parent; no specific code exists for beef siew mai with caviar topping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this specific dim sum preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Beef Siew Mai with Caviar consists of 3 pieces, each approximately 30 g, totalling approximately 90 g — a typical dim sum portion as served at Loong Dim Sum.

nutrient_source.title: Siu Mai (Steamed Pork and Shrimp Dumpling) and Beef Dumpling — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; adapted for beef siew mai with caviar topping using USDA beef dumpling and caviar entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'beef siew mai with caviar' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA FDC entry for steamed beef dumplings (FDC ID 2097663, 'Beef dumplings, steamed') as the primary base, providing the core macro profile; (2) USDA FDC entry for caviar (FDC ID 174193) to account for the caviar topping (~5% by weight of the dumpling). The resulting composite per-100g values reflect a wagyu-style beef filling in a thin wheat wrapper topped with fish roe. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat-based steamed dumplings and dim sum — analogous estimate based on steamed wheat dumpling (jiaozi/dim sum) GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for beef siew mai with caviar. GI estimated at 45 based on steamed wheat-wrapper dumplings (jiaozi/dim sum type) from the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values of approximately 40–50 for steamed wheat-based dumplings with meat fillings. The thin wheat wrapper and high protein/fat content from beef and caviar lower the effective GI relative to plain wheat products. GL per serving calculated as: (45 × 14.0 g carbohydrate per 100g × 90g serving / 100) / 100 = 5.7 g. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100g; ~432 mg per 3-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of beef siew mai with caviar, as a single 3-piece serving provides approximately 432 mg sodium — nearly 19% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — primarily from soy sauce seasoning and caviar, and should be balanced against other sodium sources in the meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate content (~14 g per 100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Beef siew mai with caviar has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 45 and a glycemic load of approximately 5.7 per 3-piece serving, making it a relatively suitable dim sum choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderation as part of a balanced meal; however, the sodium content from soy-based seasoning warrants attention.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.8 g per 100g) and elevated sodium; caviar contributes cholesterol and omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume beef siew mai with caviar in moderation, as the saturated fat from beef (~3.8 g/100g) and elevated sodium (~480 mg/100g) are cardiovascular risk factors, though the caviar topping provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids that may partially offset lipid-related risk when overall dietary pattern is heart-healthy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Siew Mai with Caviar is a premium Cantonese dim sum item served at Loong Dim Sum in Singapore, consisting of open-topped steamed dumplings made with a thin wheat flour wrapper encasing a seasoned wagyu-style minced beef filling, topped with a small portion of caviar (fish roe). The dish is served in a portion of 3 pieces (~90 g total). The beef filling is seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, and cornstarch, giving it a tender, juicy texture reminiscent of wagyu beef, while the caviar topping adds a briny, umami-rich burst of flavour and a luxurious visual presentation. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 198 kcal, 13.5 g protein, 10.2 g fat (of which 3.8 g is saturated), and 14 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of ~480 mg per 100g from soy-based seasoning and caviar. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI), making it a relatively moderate-impact carbohydrate food. The caviar topping contributes vitamin B12, omega-3 fatty acids, and selenium. Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, while the low GI and high protein content make it a more suitable dim sum option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes compared to high-GI alternatives. This dish is not halal-certified due to the beef and caviar combination in a restaurant context, and contains allergens including gluten (wheat wrapper), fish (caviar), soy, and sesame.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for 'beef siew mai with caviar' was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA FDC data for steamed beef dumplings and caviar. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for steamed wheat-wrapper dumplings with meat fillings. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FDC SR Legacy entries for steamed beef dumplings (FDC ID 2097663) and caviar (FDC ID 174193); no exact HPB or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific dish. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~45) based on steamed wheat-wrapper dim sum dumplings from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for beef siew mai with caviar was found. 5. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant (Loong Dim Sum) does not carry a MUIS halal certification; consumers should verify independently. 6. The caviar topping is assumed to be a small garnish (~5% by weight), consistent with typical dim sum presentation; actual caviar quantity may vary by restaurant preparation. 7. Price is SGD 26.00 for 3 pieces, reflecting the premium positioning of this dish. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the composite USDA FDC source and are provided as best-available approximations.
